name: Chaophraya Dam Golf Course
source_notes: EXISTENCE UNCERTAIN. The Chaophraya Dam (เขื่อนเจ้าพระยา) in Chai Nat is a run-of-river barrage/weir with no hydroelectric generation and no significant EGAT township compound — it is not the same type of facility as Bhumibol, Sirikit, Ubolratana, or Rajjaprabha. It is very unlikely to have an 18-hole golf course. Possible explanations: (1) this entry is a misidentification or data error in the original directory, (2) it refers to a different dam/reservoir entirely (e.g., Sirikit Dam in Uttaradit, which does have an EGAT compound), (3) it refers to a civilian course near the Chaophraya River named 'Chaophraya'. Province unknown. No data found in any source.
skip_reason: EXISTENCE_UNCERTAIN — Chaophraya Dam is a run-of-river weir, not a large hydroelectric compound; no golf course data found in any source
